methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Mbodi AI is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Xavier Chi and Sebastian Peralta. The company has raised $4.4M to date. The valuation universe for robotics and manufacturing comprises 9 private companies and 1 public company, with notable private peers including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

Given the nascent stage and lack of sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with aligned valuation tiers, a peer-median approach was not applicable; only one triangulation method—stage-progression—yielded reliable output. The stage-progression method applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement to the $4.4M raised baseline, implying a valuation of $11M. This single-method result constrains confidence but establishes a directional anchor.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $9M to $13M, with a median of $11M. The primary uncertainty stems from reliance on a single methodology and the sparse comparable set at Mbodi's stage and sector specificity. Validating this range would benefit from: (1) deeper operational metrics (revenue run-rate, customer traction, unit economics) not present in the current data; and (2) additional Seed-stage peers in robotics with transparent valuations. Investors should treat the $11M median as illustrative rather than definitive until tighter comparable data emerges.
